extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that setting sweating=true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. It is VIOLATED by the interaction_drop rule.

sweating appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest priority in the system), which fires only when all ten conjuncts are true, including sweating, and sets acuity := self_care (the lowest level).

Counterexample pair (all other inputs fixed): syncope=abdominal_pain=focal_weakness=stiff_neck=dizziness=palpitations=dyspnea=pallor=rash=true, vomiting=false.

- With sweating=FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire (sweating false). The highest-priority matching rules are priority-3 emergent rules (raise_pallor, raise_abdominal_pain, raise_stiff_neck, raise_palpitations), so acuity = emergent.
- With sweating=TRUE: now all ten conjuncts of interaction_drop hold, so it fires at priority 100, beating every other rule (max other priority = 3), setting acuity = self_care.

Acuity drops from emergent to self_care when sweating is set true, which lowers acuity and violates monotonicity. The witness_assignment given is the sweating=true endpoint that yields the lowered acuity (self_care); the comparison point is the identical input with sweating=false (emergent).
